trip.name: Chill Foodie Adventure in Hong Kong

days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: First Taste of the City

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Touchdown at HKIA. Collect your luggage and follow signs to the Airport Express Train for quick transport to Central Station.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Lantau Island, HK

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel ICON
timeline.detail: Relax and refresh at this luxurious hotel, located just 5 minutes on foot from Tsim Sha Tsui MTR station. Enjoy stunning views of Victoria Harbour.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Victoria Harbour Promenade Walk
timeline.detail: Stroll along the Tsim Sha Tsui promenade, enjoying the skyline views of Hong Kong Island. Perfect for photos and sunset watching.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Indulge in traditional wonton noodles at this acclaimed establishment. Don't miss the flavorful broth and tender dumplings.

timeline.title: Hong Kong Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Experience the nightly light show featuring over 40 buildings on both sides of Victoria Harbour. Best viewed from the promenade.

timeline.title: Nightcap at Aqua Spirit
timeline.detail: Enjoy a cocktail with stunning views of the city skyline at this stylish rooftop bar. A perfect way to end your first night.
timeline.mapQuery: Aqua Spirit, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

localTips: Purchase an Octopus Card at the airport for easy travel on public transport.
Try to catch the light show from the Tsim Sha Tsui waterfront for the best experience.
Weather can be humid; dress comfortably and stay hydrated.

days.title: Exploring Hong Kong Island
days.subtitle: Culture and Experiences

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a hearty Hong Kong breakfast of milk tea and macaroni soup at this famous eatery. Expect a busy, bustling atmosphere.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Take the Peak Tram
timeline.detail: Ride the iconic Peak Tram to Victoria Peak for breathtaking views of Hong Kong. Arrive early to avoid long queues.

timeline.title: Victoria Peak Views
timeline.detail: Enjoy panoramic views from the Peak, capturing the iconic skyline and surrounding islands. Perfect opportunity for photography.

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Savor the world-famous dim sum at this Michelin-starred restaurant. Arrive early for optimal seating and avoid long wait times.
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Experience the vibrant culture of Hong Kong at this famous temple known for its fortune-telling and beautiful architecture.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shopping in Causeway Bay
timeline.detail: Explore the bustling shopping district of Causeway Bay, filled with restaurants, shops, and local street food carts. A shopping paradise!

localTips: Tim Ho Wan is very popular; consider visiting during off-peak hours.
Bring your own water bottle; many places will fill it for you.
Check for sales in Causeway Bay for good deals on brands.

days.title: Markets and Departure
days.subtitle: Taste of Local Life

timeline.title: Breakfast at a Local Cha Chaan Teng
timeline.detail: Sample classic Hong Kong breakfast dishes such as French toast and baked rice in this cozy local café. Enjoy the lively atmosphere.
timeline.mapQuery: Cha Chaan Teng, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit the Temple Street Night Market
timeline.detail: Wander through the bustling market filled with stalls selling everything from clothes to local snacks. Great for souvenirs and local eats.
timeline.mapQuery: Temple Street Night Market, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Check out Jade Market
timeline.detail: Explore the Jade Market, filled with vendors selling jewelry and artifacts. A cultural hub where you can find meaningful souvenirs.

timeline.title: Lunch at Sister's Kitchen
timeline.detail: Enjoy heartwarming local dishes at Sister's Kitchen in Mong Kok. This spot is famous for its claypot rice and home-style cooking.
timeline.mapQuery: Sister's Kitchen, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Last-minute shopping in Mong Kok
timeline.detail: Grab any last-minute souvenirs or gifts. Explore the series of shops, including sneaker stores and fashion boutiques.

timeline.title: Return to Airport
timeline.detail: Make your way back to the airport via Airport Express. Ensure to arrive at least 2 hours before your flight.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Lantau Island, HK

localTips: Make sure to haggle at markets for the best prices.
Keep your belongings secure in busy areas.
Consider sending souvenirs via post if you're overweight for your flight.

hotels.name: Hotel ICON
hotels.note: 5-min walk from Tsim Sha Tsui station, stunning views.
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Rd, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Average RM 100 per meal for 2 days
Includes Peak Tram and entrance fees
